trial.full_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Evaluating the Efficacy of Axicabtagene Ciloleucel Versus Standard of Care Therapy in Subjects With Relapsed/Refractory Diffuse Large B Cell Lymphoma
trial.indication: Second-line therapy for relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma
primary_references: Locke FL, Miklos DB, Jacobson CA, et al. Axicabtagene Ciloleucel as Second-Line Therapy for Large B-Cell Lymphoma. N Engl J Med. 2022;386(7):640-654.
trial.notes_for_agent: ZUMA-7 is specifically a 2nd-line trial for patients who are refractory to or relapsed within 12 months of completing first-line chemoimmunotherapy. Patients in 3L+ setting should be evaluated against ZUMA-1 criteria instead. Prior autoSCT is an EXCLUSION for ZUMA-7 (it was the comparator arm).

agent_guidance.evaluation_approach: Evaluate each criterion independently. For each: determine MET, NOT_MET, or INDETERMINATE. Cite evidence as {document_id, quoted_text_span}. Prefer INDETERMINATE over a wrong answer.
agent_guidance.final_determination_logic: ELIGIBLE requires ALL inclusion criteria MET and ZERO exclusion criteria MET. If any criterion is INDETERMINATE, final result is NEEDS_REVIEW with missing data flagged. If any inclusion is NOT_MET or any exclusion is MET, result is INELIGIBLE.
agent_guidance.unstructured_data_priority: Pathology reports, progress notes, and treatment histories often contain information absent from structured fields (problem lists, billing codes). Always read narrative content. Do not rely solely on ICD codes.
agent_guidance.confidence_calibration: HIGH when evidence is explicit and recent. MEDIUM when inferable from multiple documents. LOW when requiring extrapolation or when documentation is stale.

recency_rules.labs: must be within 28 days of evaluation
recency_rules.imaging: most recent PET/CT or MRI defining disease status
recency_rules.infectious_serologies: within 6 months or as clinically indicated

eligible_histologies.name: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, not otherwise specified
High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma arising from follicular lymphoma
aliases: Richter-like transformation of FL to DLBCL

ineligible_histologies.name: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia / small lymphocytic lymphoma
Richter's transformation from CLL
ineligible_histologies.reason: Explicit exclusion — histology is DLBCL-like but CLL origin disqualifies

ineligible_histologies.name: Mantle cell lymphoma
ineligible_histologies.reason: Not in eligible histology list for ZUMA-7

ineligible_histologies.name: Primary mediastinal B-cell lymphoma
ineligible_histologies.reason: Not in ZUMA-7 histology list. NOTE: PMBCL IS eligible for axi-cel per ZUMA-1 (3L+ indication) — consider alternative trial

ineligible_histologies.name: Classical or nodular lymphocyte-predominant Hodgkin lymphoma

inclusion_criteria.name: Adult age
inclusion_criteria.description: Age ≥18 years at time of consent
look_in: any document with DOB or age
common_pitfalls: Pediatric patients should be evaluated for pediatric trials instead

inclusion_criteria.name: Eligible histologic diagnosis
inclusion_criteria.description: Histologically confirmed large B-cell lymphoma of an eligible subtype (see eligible_histologies list)
inclusion_criteria.met_if: Pathology report documents a diagnosis matching one entry in eligible_histologies
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Diagnosis matches an entry in ineligible_histologies
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: Histology documented only as 'lymphoma NOS' or 'non-Hodgkin lymphoma' without specific subtype; or ICD code is unspecific (e.g., C85.90) and no pathology narrative available
common_pitfalls: ICD/billing codes are frequently unspecific (C85.90 = 'lymphoma, unspecified'). Always read pathology narrative.
Transformed FL must be documented as transformed to DLBCL, not just FL with large cells
Double-hit / HGBCL IS eligible — do not exclude based on high-grade features
T-cell immunophenotype (CD3+, CD20-) rules out eligibility even if morphology looks large-cell
PMBCL is NOT eligible for ZUMA-7 but IS eligible for ZUMA-1
inclusion_criteria.required_evidence: Pathology report with explicit diagnosis + supportive IHC (CD20+, B-cell markers)

inclusion_criteria.name: Relapsed or refractory after first-line therapy
inclusion_criteria.description: Disease is refractory to first-line therapy OR relapsed within 12 months of completing first-line therapy
inclusion_criteria.refractory_definition: No complete response to first-line therapy. Specifically: best response of progressive disease, stable disease, or partial response followed by progression. NOT refractory if CR achieved.
inclusion_criteria.relapse_window: Documented relapse within 12 months of completing last cycle of first-line therapy
inclusion_criteria.met_if: Patient meets refractory definition OR relapsed ≤12 months from end of 1L
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Patient achieved CR to 1L and relapsed >12 months after completion (late relapse — eligible for SOC autoSCT path, not ZUMA-7)
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: End-of-treatment response not documented; relapse date unclear
common_pitfalls: Interim PET CR followed by end-of-treatment PD = refractory (not responsive)
Partial response that later progresses during tx or within 12 mo = refractory
Must calculate relapse interval from END of 1L therapy, not start
If pt has had 2 prior lines, they are in 3L setting — evaluate for ZUMA-1 instead
inclusion_criteria.required_evidence: Treatment dates for 1L regimen + response assessment (PET/CT reports, response documentation) + date of relapse/progression

inclusion_criteria.name: First-line regimen contained anti-CD20 + anthracycline
inclusion_criteria.description: Prior first-line therapy must have contained an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody AND an anthracycline-containing chemotherapy regimen
common_qualifying_regimens: Pola-R-CHP (polatuzumab replacing vincristine but contains R + doxo)
inclusion_criteria.met_if: 1L regimen includes BOTH an anti-CD20 agent AND an anthracycline
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: 1L lacked either anti-CD20 (e.g., CHOP alone, bendamustine-based) or anthracycline (e.g., R-Benda alone, R-Lenalidomide)
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: Regimen documented only by acronym not in common_qualifying_regimens list, or components unclear
common_pitfalls: 'Rituximab + CHOP' in free text = R-CHOP (same regimen, parse this)
'DA-R-EPOCH' contains doxorubicin → qualifies
'R-Benda' / 'BR' (bendamustine-rituximab) does NOT contain anthracycline → does not qualify for this criterion
Pola-R-CHP replaces vincristine with polatuzumab but retains doxorubicin → qualifies
CD20-negative tumors may have waiver — check pathology CD20 status
inclusion_criteria.required_evidence: Treatment log or progress note specifying regimen components

inclusion_criteria.name: ECOG performance status 0-1
inclusion_criteria.description: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status of 0 or 1
inclusion_criteria.met_if: ECOG 0 or 1 explicitly documented in recent visit
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: ECOG 2, 3, or 4 documented OR narrative describes function consistent with ECOG ≥2 (e.g., bed-bound >50% of day, requires assistance with ADLs, wheelchair-dependent for non-medical use)
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: No ECOG documented AND narrative is insufficient to infer (e.g., 'patient doing reasonably well' without functional detail)

ecog_definitions.0: Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction
ecog_definitions.1: Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature
ecog_definitions.2: Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities; up and about more than 50% of waking hours
ecog_definitions.3: Capable of only limited self-care; confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours
ecog_definitions.4: Completely disabled; cannot carry on any self-care; totally confined to bed or chair

common_pitfalls: Absence of documented ECOG does not mean patient is ECOG 0 — check narrative
Phrases like 'bed-bound most of the day', 'requires assistance with ADLs', 'wheelchair-dependent' suggest ECOG 3-4
Phrases like 'works full-time', 'runs regularly', 'independent ADLs' suggest ECOG 0-1
'Uses walker intermittently' alone is not decisive — look at overall function
inclusion_criteria.required_evidence: Recent progress note (within 4 weeks) with ECOG or functional description

inclusion_criteria.name: Adequate absolute neutrophil count
inclusion_criteria.description: ANC ≥1000/μL (or ≥1.0 x10⁹/L) without G-CSF support in prior 7 days
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: ANC < 1.0 x10⁹/L, or recent G-CSF (filgrastim, pegfilgrastim) administration within 7 days of the lab
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: No recent CBC or unit ambiguity
common_pitfalls: Unit conversions: 1000/μL = 1.0 x10⁹/L = 1.0 K/μL
Must check if G-CSF given recently (would artificially inflate ANC)
Borderline values (e.g., 0.95 vs 1.02) should be confirmed with repeat lab

inclusion_criteria.name: Adequate platelet count
inclusion_criteria.description: Platelets ≥75,000/μL (≥75 x10⁹/L) without transfusion in prior 7 days
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Platelets < 75 x10⁹/L, or platelet transfusion within 7 days of measurement
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: No recent CBC or transfusion history unclear
common_pitfalls: Same transfusion check applies as ANC/G-CSF

inclusion_criteria.name: Adequate renal function
inclusion_criteria.description: Creatinine clearance ≥60 mL/min (calculated or measured) or serum creatinine ≤1.5 × ULN
inclusion_criteria.met_if: CrCl ≥ 60 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault or MDRD) OR Cr ≤ 1.5 × institutional ULN
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: Only Cr available without age/weight for CrCl calculation and value is borderline
common_pitfalls: Older patients with reduced muscle mass may have 'normal' Cr but low CrCl — always calculate
Use Cockcroft-Gault: ((140-age) × weight_kg × (0.85 if female)) / (72 × Cr_mg/dL)
eGFR ≥60 by MDRD/CKD-EPI is generally acceptable

inclusion_criteria.name: Adequate hepatic function
inclusion_criteria.description: ALT and AST ≤2.5 × ULN; total bilirubin ≤1.5 × ULN (≤3 × ULN if due to Gilbert's syndrome or lymphoma involvement)
inclusion_criteria.met_if: ALT ≤ 2.5×ULN AND AST ≤ 2.5×ULN AND total bili ≤ 1.5×ULN (or Gilbert's/lymphoma exception)
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Any transaminase > 2.5×ULN, or tbili > 1.5×ULN without Gilbert's/lymphoma explanation
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: LFTs not recent or institutional ULN not specified
common_pitfalls: Typical institutional ULN: ALT ~40 U/L, AST ~40 U/L, tbili ~1.2 mg/dL
Gilbert's syndrome = isolated indirect hyperbilirubinemia, must be documented
Lymphomatous liver involvement can raise LFTs — check imaging for liver lesions

inclusion_criteria.name: Adequate cardiac function
inclusion_criteria.description: Left ventricular ejection fraction ≥50% by echocardiogram or MUGA; no evidence of pericardial effusion; no clinically significant arrhythmia
inclusion_criteria.met_if: Recent LVEF ≥ 50% with no significant pericardial effusion or uncontrolled arrhythmia
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: LVEF < 50%, OR moderate-large pericardial effusion, OR NYHA class III-IV heart failure, OR uncontrolled arrhythmia
inclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: No recent echo (>6 months) or results not in chart
common_pitfalls: LVEF 45-49% is below ZUMA-7 threshold even though many CAR-T protocols accept ≥45%
Prior anthracycline exposure → always verify current EF before CAR-T
Borderline EF + prior MI may require cardio-onc clearance

inclusion_criteria.name: Intent to proceed to autologous stem cell transplant
inclusion_criteria.description: Patient is a candidate for and intended to proceed to high-dose chemotherapy + autologous stem cell transplant (this was the ZUMA-7 comparator arm standard-of-care)
inclusion_criteria.met_if: Treating oncologist has deemed patient a transplant candidate (implicit if ECOG 0-1, adequate organ function, no prior autoSCT)
inclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Patient deemed non-transplant-eligible due to age, comorbidity, or frailty
inclusion_criteria.notes: This criterion is more of a clinical judgment gate. Most patients meeting all other criteria will satisfy this implicitly. Some protocols waive this.
common_pitfalls: Do not confuse with requiring patient to HAVE had autoSCT — prior autoSCT is an EXCLUSION (see EXC-02)

exclusion_criteria.name: Prior CD19-directed therapy
exclusion_criteria.description: Any prior treatment with a CD19-targeted agent, including prior CAR-T cell therapy, CD19 bispecific antibody (blinatumomab), or antibody-drug conjugate (loncastuximab tesirine)
exclusion_criteria.met_if_patient_has_received: Any agent in excluded_agents list, at any time in history
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: No prior CD19-directed therapy in any record
common_pitfalls: Prior CAR-T may be buried in old notes or outside records — scan historical tx log
Tafasitamab + lenalidomide (Monjuvi regimen) is a prior CD19 exposure — EXCLUDES even though not CAR-T
'CAR-T evaluation' in notes is NOT same as CAR-T infusion — check if infusion actually occurred

exclusion_criteria.name: Prior autologous stem cell transplant
exclusion_criteria.description: ZUMA-7 specifically excludes patients with prior autoSCT, as autoSCT was the comparator arm. (NOTE: patients with prior autoSCT may still be eligible for axi-cel per ZUMA-1 / 3L+ label.)
exclusion_criteria.met_if: Patient has had prior autologous stem cell transplant (self-donor)
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: No prior SCT of any kind
common_pitfalls: Distinguish AUTO (self-donor) from ALLO (different donor) — both mentioned as 'SCT' or 'BMT'
BEAM, CBV, melphalan-based conditioning regimens typically precede autoSCT
If patient has had autoSCT, consider routing to ZUMA-1 (3L+) evaluation instead of disqualifying entirely

exclusion_criteria.name: Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant
common_pitfalls: Prior autoSCT is also exclusionary per EXC-02, but for different reason (trial design)
Matched-sibling, matched-unrelated, haploidentical, and cord-blood transplants all count as alloSCT

exclusion_criteria.name: Central nervous system lymphoma involvement
exclusion_criteria.description: Primary CNS lymphoma OR secondary CNS involvement by lymphoma (parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or CSF-positive)
exclusion_criteria.met_if: MRI shows parenchymal lesion consistent with lymphoma, OR leptomeningeal enhancement, OR CSF cytology/flow positive for malignant cells, OR documented history of CNS lymphoma (even if treated)
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: MRI brain clear AND no CSF involvement documented AND no history of CNS disease
exclusion_criteria.indeterminate_if: MRI brain not yet done (often required for CAR-T workup)
common_pitfalls: History of CNS-directed prophylaxis (IT MTX) does not imply CNS involvement — check indication
Cranial nerve palsies or neuro sx should prompt CNS workup
MATRix/MATR regimens imply prior CNS-directed tx and may indicate CNS dz history

exclusion_criteria.name: Richter's transformation from CLL
exclusion_criteria.description: DLBCL arising as Richter's transformation from underlying CLL/SLL
exclusion_criteria.met_if: Pathology or history documents Richter's transformation, or patient has known CLL with new large-cell transformation
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: De novo DLBCL without CLL background
common_pitfalls: Histology alone looks like DLBCL — must check disease background/origin
Look for prior CLL diagnosis or lymphocytosis history
Richter's is specifically called out as exclusion despite DLBCL morphology

exclusion_criteria.name: Active HIV infection
exclusion_criteria.description: HIV positive with detectable viral load, or uncontrolled HIV
exclusion_criteria.met_if: HIV Ab/Ag positive AND viral load detectable (or CD4 < 200), OR not on suppressive ART
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: HIV negative, OR HIV+ with undetectable VL on stable ART and CD4 > 200 (some protocols allow this)
common_pitfalls: Well-controlled HIV (undetectable VL, CD4 >200) may not be absolute exclusion in all CAR-T trials — check specific protocol
Detectable viral load OR active opportunistic infection = clear exclusion
EBV+ DLBCL in HIV patients is common — tumor EBV status ≠ HIV status

exclusion_criteria.name: Active hepatitis B or C infection
exclusion_criteria.description: Active HBV infection (HBsAg+ or HBV DNA detectable) or active HCV infection (HCV RNA detectable)
exclusion_criteria.met_if: HBsAg positive with detectable HBV DNA, OR HCV RNA detectable
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: HBsAg negative; resolved HBV (HBsAg-, HBcAb+) may be acceptable with antiviral prophylaxis; HCV Ab+ with undetectable RNA after treatment is acceptable
common_pitfalls: HBcAb+ alone (resolved prior infection) usually requires entecavir/tenofovir prophylaxis but not exclusion
Check HBV DNA, not just HBsAg — reactivation risk
HCV Ab+ post-treatment with SVR is not an exclusion

exclusion_criteria.name: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression
exclusion_criteria.description: Personal history of autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (steroids >10 mg prednisone equivalent, biologics, DMARDs, or other immunosuppressants) within the last 2 years
exclusion_criteria.met_if: PATIENT has documented autoimmune disease currently or recently (2 years) requiring systemic immunosuppression
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: No personal history of active autoimmune disease; family history of autoimmune conditions does NOT qualify
common_pitfalls: FAMILY history ≠ personal history — do not flag patient for relatives' autoimmune conditions
Hashimoto's on levothyroxine alone is generally NOT exclusionary (thyroid replacement ≠ immunosuppression)
Type 1 diabetes on insulin alone is generally NOT exclusionary
Vitiligo, psoriasis not on systemic tx generally NOT exclusionary
Topical or inhaled steroids alone generally do not count as systemic immunosuppression

exclusion_criteria.name: Uncontrolled active infection
exclusion_criteria.description: Any active uncontrolled infection requiring IV antibiotics, antifungals, or antivirals
exclusion_criteria.met_if: Currently on IV antimicrobials for active infection, OR uncontrolled infection without clear resolution pathway
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: No active infection, or infection treated and resolved
common_pitfalls: Prophylactic antimicrobials (e.g., acyclovir, Bactrim) do NOT count as active infection
Recent infection treated to resolution is acceptable
Active sepsis, pneumonia, bacteremia are disqualifying until resolved

exclusion_criteria.name: Other active malignancy within 3 years
exclusion_criteria.description: History of another invasive malignancy within the last 3 years, except for non-melanoma skin cancer or in-situ cervical carcinoma adequately treated
exclusion_criteria.met_if: Another active or recently (within 3 years) treated invasive malignancy
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: No other cancer, OR other cancer >3 years in remission, OR non-melanoma skin cancer, OR CIS adequately treated
common_pitfalls: Prior transformed lymphoma (e.g., FL that transformed to DLBCL) is SAME disease — not a second malignancy
Basal cell / squamous cell skin cancers generally allowed
DCIS and cervical CIS generally allowed
Early-stage treated prostate cancer on active surveillance: variable — check protocol specifics

exclusion_criteria.name: Clinically significant cardiac disease
exclusion_criteria.description: NYHA class III-IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 12 months, or clinically significant uncontrolled arrhythmia
exclusion_criteria.met_if: Any of: NYHA III-IV HF, unstable angina, MI within 12 months, uncontrolled arrhythmia, symptomatic aortic stenosis
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Stable cardiac disease, EF ≥50%, no recent events
common_pitfalls: Old MI (>12 months ago) with preserved EF is generally acceptable
Atrial fibrillation on rate/rhythm control is generally acceptable
Low-EF heart failure is both a cardiac exclusion AND fails INC-11

exclusion_criteria.name: Pregnancy or lactation
exclusion_criteria.not_met_if: Not pregnant (negative serum/urine hCG in women of childbearing potential); post-menopausal; surgically sterile; male
exclusion_criteria.required_evidence: Negative pregnancy test within 7 days for women of childbearing potential

alternative_trial_suggestions.if_prior_autoSCT_or_3L_plus: Consider ZUMA-1 criteria (axi-cel 3L+ label): DLBCL, PMBCL, HGBCL, tFL after ≥2 prior lines. Most ZUMA-7 criteria apply but prior autoSCT is ALLOWED and ≥2 prior lines is REQUIRED.
alternative_trial_suggestions.if_PMBCL: PMBCL is not eligible for ZUMA-7 but IS eligible for ZUMA-1 (axi-cel 3L+) and various liso-cel trials.
alternative_trial_suggestions.if_primary_CNS_lymphoma: Consider CNS-specific CAR-T trials (expanded access or investigational); standard ZUMA-7/TRANSFORM/BELINDA exclude CNS disease.
alternative_trial_suggestions.if_CLL_Richters: Consider CLL-specific CAR-T trials (liso-cel has CLL indication per TRANSCEND-CLL-004).
alternative_trial_suggestions.if_liso_cel_preferred: TRANSFORM trial uses similar 2L eligibility with liso-cel; broader age range (no upper limit) and allows ECOG 2 in some cases.
alternative_trial_suggestions.if_tisa_cel_preferred: BELINDA trial for tisa-cel in 2L failed primary endpoint — tisa-cel 2L not standard; tisa-cel approved 3L+ per JULIET.

evidence.quoted_text: exact span from document supporting determination

flags: list of concerns, missing data, or recommendations
alternative_trials_suggested: list of trial IDs if patient fails ZUMA-7 but may fit elsewhere
